skill.name: labarchive-integration
skill.description: Automates LabArchives electronic lab notebook workflows, including API authentication, notebook backups, entry creation, attachment uploads, and integration planning.
skill.summary: Use LabArchives APIs to manage notebooks, entries, attachments, backups, reports, and research tool integrations.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but most external-command, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and ransomware alerts are false positives from Markdown examples, API names, and security guidance text. Real risk remains because the skill includes executable scripts that collect LabArchives credentials, contact configurable API endpoints, upload local files, and write notebook backups containing sensitive research data.

medium_findings.title: Credential Collection and Local Secret File
medium_findings.description: The setup script prompts for institutional API credentials and a user external applications password, then writes them to config.yaml. It sets mode 600, but the file remains a local secret that can be exposed through backups, logs, or repository commits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly collects credential values and writes them to disk. The permissions mitigation is present, so this is a managed but real secret-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Configurable API Endpoint Receives Credentials
medium_findings.description: The setup workflow accepts a custom API endpoint, and later scripts send access credentials and user authentication data through the configured client or upload request. A malicious or mistaken endpoint could receive LabArchives credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The custom endpoint is explicit, and the configured API URL is used for authenticated requests. Legitimate regional endpoints are supported, but endpoint validation is not enforced.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Notebook Backup Writes
medium_findings.description: The notebook backup script downloads notebook content and attachments, creates an output directory, and writes the response to local files. This can store regulated or confidential research data outside LabArchives controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code writes API response content to local archive or metadata files. This is intended behavior but creates a data exposure risk if output locations are not protected.

medium_findings.title: Local File Upload to LabArchives
medium_findings.description: The entry operations script reads user-selected files and uploads them to the configured LabArchives endpoint with access credentials. Incorrect paths, broad batch directories, or wrong notebook IDs can expose local files to an unintended notebook.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload behavior is direct and intentional. It is legitimate for this skill, but users must verify file selections and destination notebook identifiers.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized HTML Content Submitted to Entries
medium_findings.description: The create-entry command accepts HTML-supported content and wraps plain text in paragraph tags without sanitization. If LabArchives renders stored HTML, unsafe markup could create stored content or script injection risk depending on platform filtering.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script permits raw HTML in user-supplied content. The downstream platform may sanitize it, so impact is uncertain without LabArchives rendering guarantees.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shows Disabled TLS Verification
low_findings.description: The authentication guide includes a troubleshooting example that disables TLS verification for testing. The text warns against production use, but users could copy it into real workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe option is present only in documentation and is labeled as not recommended for production. Copy-paste misuse remains a minor risk.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged many Markdown inline code spans and shell examples as Ruby backtick execution. I did not find evidence of subprocess, os.system, popen, eval, or exec execution in the executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation fences, inline code, URLs, or command examples. A targeted search found no Python external command execution primitives in scripts.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Ransomware Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and ransomware-style static hits map to words such as password, OAuth, security guidance, or encryption advice rather than cryptographic implementation or destructive behavior. No evidence found for malicious encryption, deletion, or extortion logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced areas describe authentication and secure storage practices. The executable scripts do not implement encryption routines or destructive file operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Requests with Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts authenticate to LabArchives and upload attachments using configured access credentials. This pattern is necessary for the skill but should be limited to trusted endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential-bearing network calls are directly visible. The behavior matches the skill purpose and is not malicious by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Secret and Backup File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts write credential configuration and notebook backups to local disk. These files can contain secrets or sensitive research data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are explicit and security-relevant. The config file permission hardening reduces but does not remove exposure risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Upload Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: Attachment commands read a user-provided file or all files in a user-provided directory and upload them to a notebook entry.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are user-selected and uploads are intentional. There is no hidden exfiltration, but operator error can expose data.

content.value_statement: Research teams need reliable ways to move data into and out of electronic lab notebooks. This skill provides LabArchives API workflows for authentication, backups, entries, attachments, reports, and integrations.
actual_capabilities: Guides LabArchives API credential setup and regional endpoint selection.
Lists accessible notebooks and retrieves user access information through API calls.
Backs up notebooks as archives or metadata-only XML and JSON outputs.
Creates notebook entries, comments, and attachment uploads from local files.
Documents workflows for Jupyter, Protocols.io, REDCap, GraphPad Prism, SnapGene, and related tools.
Explains site report endpoints for enterprise usage and compliance reporting.
limitations: Requires Enterprise LabArchives API access and valid institutional credentials.
Uses local config files unless users adapt the scripts for environment variables or secret managers.
Does not validate custom API endpoints beyond accepting user input.
Does not sanitize HTML content before submitting entry text.

use_cases.title: Back Up Research Notebooks
use_cases.description: Download notebook archives or metadata snapshots for continuity, audits, and controlled local retention.

use_cases.title: Upload Experiment Outputs
use_cases.description: Create entries and attach CSV, image, document, or instrument files after an experiment run.

use_cases.title: Connect Analysis Tools
use_cases.description: Plan workflows that move Jupyter, REDCap, Protocols.io, and analysis outputs into LabArchives records.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up API Access
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a first LabArchives API workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure LabArchives API access for the US endpoint. List the credentials I need, where they come from, and how to protect config.yaml.

prompt_templates.title: Back Up One Notebook
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple operational task for a known notebook ID.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a command for backing up notebook NBID into a protected backup folder. Include the difference between full and metadata-only backups.

prompt_templates.title: Upload Experiment Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an ELN entry and attach local result files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a LabArchives entry upload workflow for an experiment folder. Include title, entry text, attachment handling, and checks before uploading files.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Integration Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a LabArchives integration that exports Jupyter analysis results and REDCap summaries into notebook entries. Include authentication, metadata, audit trail, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: I need to back up a notebook without attachments for compliance review.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the notebook backup workflow, recommends metadata-only output, explains where the backup will be written, and reminds the user to protect the destination folder.

output_examples.input: I want to upload a CSV and image to an existing experiment entry.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the attachment upload steps, verifies notebook and entry identifiers, checks local file paths, and warns against uploading unrelated files.

output_examples.input: Our lab wants to connect Jupyter notebooks to LabArchives records.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines conversion to HTML or PDF, entry creation, original notebook attachment, environment metadata, and API authentication requirements.

best_practices: Use environment variables or a managed secret store instead of committing config.yaml.
Test uploads and backups in a nonproduction notebook before using regulated research data.
Verify notebook IDs, entry IDs, file paths, and regional endpoints before every batch operation.
anti_patterns: Do not point the API URL at an untrusted custom endpoint.
Do not batch upload broad directories without reviewing every file first.
Do not paste unsanitized HTML from unknown sources into notebook entries.

faq.question: Does this skill require LabArchives Enterprise access?
faq.answer: Yes. The API workflows require Enterprise API access and credentials from a LabArchives administrator.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with scripts and references usable from each environment.

faq.question: Does it store credentials?
faq.answer: The setup script can write credentials to config.yaml. Users should protect that file or adapt the workflow to a secret manager.

faq.question: Can it upload attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. The entry operations script uploads selected files or files from a selected directory to an existing entry.

faq.question: Does it create notebook backups?
faq.answer: Yes. It can request full notebook archives or metadata-only XML and JSON backups through the LabArchives API.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it handles credentials, network requests, uploads, and sensitive notebook backups.
